testcase.name: Can_recover_known_distributional_parameters_from_unscaled_variance_GSF_model
testcase.failure: Expected `all(dat$q01 <= true_values)` to be TRUE. Differences: `actual`: FALSE `expected`: TRUE

testcase.name: SurvivalExponential_can_recover_true_parameter_including_covariates_
testcase.error: <assertError/simpleError/error/condition> Error: `length(slope_sigma)` should be equal to the number of arms Backtrace: ▆ 1. └─jmpost::SimJointData(...) at test-SurvivalExponential.R:26:5 2. ├─jmpost::sampleSubjects(longitudinal, subjects_df = baseline) 3. └─jmpost:::sampleSubjects.SimLongitudinalRandomSlope(...) 4. └─assertthat::assert_that(...)

testcase.name: SurvivalGamma_can_recover_known_values
testcase.error: <assertError/simpleError/error/condition> Error: `length(slope_sigma)` should be equal to the number of arms Backtrace: ▆ 1. └─jmpost::SimJointData(...) at test-SurvivalGamma.R:53:5 2. ├─jmpost::sampleSubjects(longitudinal, subjects_df = baseline) 3. └─jmpost:::sampleSubjects.SimLongitudinalRandomSlope(...) 4. └─assertthat::assert_that(...)

testcase.name: SurvivalLogLogistic_can_recover_known_values
testcase.error: <assertError/simpleError/error/condition> Error: `length(slope_sigma)` should be equal to the number of arms Backtrace: ▆ 1. └─jmpost::SimJointData(...) at test-SurvivalLoglogistic.R:37:5 2. ├─jmpost::sampleSubjects(longitudinal, subjects_df = baseline) 3. └─jmpost:::sampleSubjects.SimLongitudinalRandomSlope(...) 4. └─assertthat::assert_that(...)

testcase.name: SurvivalWeibullPH_can_recover_known_values
testcase.error: <assertError/simpleError/error/condition> Error: `length(slope_sigma)` should be equal to the number of arms Backtrace: ▆ 1. └─jmpost::SimJointData(...) at test-SurvivalWeibullPH.R:44:5 2. ├─jmpost::sampleSubjects(longitudinal, subjects_df = baseline) 3. └─jmpost:::sampleSubjects.SimLongitudinalRandomSlope(...) 4. └─assertthat::assert_that(...)

testcase.name: simulate_works_with_lambda_censor
testcase.error: <vctrs_error_subscript_oob/vctrs_error_subscript/rlang_error/error/condition> Error in `results@survival[, c("subject", "time", "event")]`: Can't subset columns that don't exist. x Column `subject` doesn't exist. Backtrace: ▆ 1. ├─dplyr::left_join(...) at test-simulate.R:162:5 2. ├─results@survival[, c("subject", "time", "event")] 3. ├─tibble:::`[.tbl_df`(results@survival, , c("subject", "time", "event")) 4. │ └─tibble:::vectbl_as_col_location(...) 5. │ ├─tibble:::subclass_col_index_errors(...) 6. │ │ └─base::withCallingHandlers(...) 7. │ └─vctrs::vec_as_location(j, n, names, missing = "error", call = call) 8. └─vctrs (local) `<fn>`() at vctrs/R/subscript-loc.R:84:3 9. └─vctrs:::stop_subscript_oob(...) 10. └─vctrs:::stop_subscript(...) at vctrs/R/subscript-loc.R:503:3 11. └─rlang::abort(...) at vctrs/R/subscript.R:150:3
